cleanupControlRoom.title: Maintenance Cleanup + Operations Control Room
cleanupControlRoom.why_it_matters: This is the first maintenance cycle that treats source-of-truth repair, design/UX review, deployment reality, and PM-system sync as one integrated product operation.
cleanupControlRoom.summary: The cleanup program reconciles repo truth, Notion, Linear, Vercel, design-system claims, and the dashboard itself while building an operator cockpit to keep the system honest going forward.

timeline.label: Audit + truth alignment
timeline.summary: Canonical repo truth established and obvious external drift corrected.

timeline.label: Control room shell
timeline.summary: Dashboard upgraded into an operator cockpit for delivery, UX, source health, and planning intelligence.

timeline.label: Case study monitoring
timeline.summary: Cleanup monitoring promoted into the PM shared layer so the process can become a showcase later on.

improvement_hypotheses: Live source-truth scoring will reduce future dashboard/Notion/Linear drift.
A control-room UI should shorten the time between audit, decision, and follow-up action.
Case-study instrumentation should begin during maintenance cycles, not after they finish.

skills_framework.role: Hub orchestration, phase logic, external sync, and shared-layer governance
Cross-source inspection of repo docs, framework state, and external-tool claims
UI review framing, surface-level quality assessment, and audit logic
Design-system governance, visual-token truth, and Figma handoff planning
Dashboard implementation, code review, and technical integration planning
Process instrumentation model, KPI framing, and future dashboard telemetry plan
Failure-pattern framing and feedback-loop planning for future post-launch learning
Launch-readiness review for website, App Store assets, and external-facing proof
Vercel topology review and system-health / source-health framing

success_cases.title: Cross-system truth moved closer to the codebase
success_cases.evidence: Linear and Notion were updated where repo truth was unambiguous, reducing obvious drift.

success_cases.title: The dashboard gained a real control-room shell
success_cases.evidence: A new operator surface now presents delivery truth, UX review, planning intelligence, and case-study framing in one place.

success_cases.title: The maintenance program is now itself documented as a tracked workstream
success_cases.evidence: The cleanup is represented in Linear, Notion, repo docs, and dashboard case-study data.

failure_cases.title: Status drift became systemic
failure_cases.evidence: Repo, dashboard data, Notion, Linear, and public-facing docs all diverged enough to create false confidence.

failure_cases.title: Marketing and deployment claims outpaced reality
failure_cases.evidence: The Vercel production surface is still the dashboard while docs describe the marketing site as live.

failure_cases.title: Design-system closure was overstated
failure_cases.evidence: Governance docs claimed zero raw color literals despite remaining hardcoded brand UI surfaces.

next_checkpoints: Hydrate the control room with live Notion, Linear, GitHub, and analytics data.
Replace narrative-only Claude/Figma zones with executable workflow integrations.
Track auth cleanup and marketing deployment cleanup as the next measurable before/after study.

controlCenterAlignment.title: Control Center Alignment + IA Refresh
controlCenterAlignment.why_it_matters: This is the first control-center cycle that turns the dashboard into a routed, multi-workspace operating surface instead of a single long mixed-purpose screen.
controlCenterAlignment.summary: The alignment program cleans stale roadmap truth, promotes shared PM data ahead of static fallback data, moves knowledge and case studies into dedicated spaces, and adds focused Claude, Codex, and Figma workspaces.

success_cases.title: Shared PM data becomes the first-class dashboard authority
success_cases.evidence: The new routed control center favors framework-manifest, feature-registry, task-queue, external-sync-status, and case-study-monitoring ahead of local fallback data.

success_cases.title: Knowledge and case studies become explicit product surfaces
success_cases.evidence: Reading, research, and retrospective work are no longer buried inside the control room body.

failure_cases.title: The original control room mixed too many jobs in one view
failure_cases.evidence: Operational signals, document reading, case studies, and future workflow surfaces all lived in one scroll, which made the system harder to read.

failure_cases.title: Static fallback data still carried stale planning truth
failure_cases.evidence: The dashboard still relied on outdated roadmap paths and local feature metadata that had drifted from the shared PM layer.

next_checkpoints: Sync the new production IA and route structure back into Notion and Linear.
Reconcile the updated dashboard truth into Notion and Linear so the new workspaces are reflected externally.
Decide when Claude, Codex, and Figma workspaces should evolve from action surfaces into executable integrations.
